user_query:
Help me rotate credentials for two internal services. Read the following inputs and then generate new secrets plus a validation report.

Read these files under input/:
- input/policies.json (per-service password policy)
- input/accounts.csv (columns: account_id,service,username,reserve_count)
- input/passphrases.yaml (map of role -> word_count)
- input/pins.json (list of devices and requested PIN lengths)

Do the following and write all results under output/:
1) For each row in input/accounts.csv, generate reserve_count unique passwords that satisfy the policy for that service (from input/policies.json). Guarantee per-password coverage of any required character classes and exclusion of any forbidden classes. Index each account’s reserves with password_index starting at 1. Do not reuse a password anywhere.
   - Save to output/credentials.csv with this exact header: account_id,service,username,password_index,password

2) For every generated password, compute metrics and write one JSON object per line to output/metrics.jsonl with these fields: account_id, password_index, length, has_lower, has_upper, has_digit, has_symbol, max_entropy_bits, strength_score. Use these exact rules:
   - has_*: true if the password contains at least one character in that class (lowercase [a-z], uppercase [A-Z], digits [0-9], symbols = any non-alphanumeric).
   - length: number of characters.
   - max_entropy_bits: round to 2 decimals of length * log2(pool), where pool = sum of: 26 if has_lower, 26 if has_upper, 10 if has_digit, 32 if has_symbol.
   - strength_score (integer): start at 0.
     • Length points: +30 if length >= 16; else +20 if >= 12; else +10 if >= 8; else +0.
     • Character class points: +10 if has_lower; +15 if has_upper; +10 if has_digit; +20 if has_symbol.
     • Bonus: +10 if length >= 20.
     • Unique-character ratio: let unique_chars = count of distinct characters. Let ratio = floor((unique_chars*100)/length). If ratio < 40 then -15; else if ratio >= 80 then +5.
     • Common-pattern penalties (case-insensitive): for each substring among ["password","123456","qwerty","abc123","admin","letmein","welcome","monkey"] present in the password, subtract 20.
     • Repetition penalty: if any character repeats 3 or more times consecutively anywhere, subtract 10.
     • Clamp final score to [0,100].

3) Create a copy of the policy you used at output/policies_applied.json (identical JSON to input/policies.json). Also convert input/passphrases.yaml to JSON at output/passphrase_requirements.json (role -> word_count), and copy input/pins.json to output/pin_requirements.json.

4) For each role defined in input/passphrases.yaml, generate a diceware-style passphrase and write all to output/passphrases.json as a JSON object with one key per role and values as hyphen-separated lowercase words. Each passphrase must contain exactly the requested number of words.

5) For each device in input/pins.json, generate a numeric PIN of the requested length and save to output/pins.csv with header: device,pin.

6) Write output/summary.md including at least these two lines (with the actual numbers):
   - Total passwords: <N>
   - Unique passwords: <N>
Where N equals the number of rows in output/credentials.csv.

Important:
- Use only the files under input/ for reading and only write to output/.
- Do not print the generated secrets in the chat; place them only in the files.
